g.data-narration: Apache Storm is a distributed real-time computation system for processing unbounded data streams with guaranteed message processing.
Apache Storm is free, open-source software that processes data in real-time as it arrives, with sub-second latency and guaranteed delivery.
g.text: → Similar to Hadoop but for streaming

g.data-narration: Storm excels at IoT applications requiring instant analytics like equipment failure detection and security alerts.
Storm uses a master-worker architecture with Nimbus as master, Supervisors as workers, and ZooKeeper for coordination.
Nimbus is the master node that distributes code across the cluster, assigns tasks to supervisors, and monitors topology health.
Supervisors run on each worker machine, managing worker processes that execute spouts and bolts assigned by Nimbus.
g.text: • Runs on each machine in cluster
Workers: 4 | Executors per worker: 8 | Tasks per executor: 2

g.data-narration: A Storm topology is a DAG of Spouts (data sources) and Bolts (processing logic) connected by stream groupings.
Spouts are the entry points that read data from external sources like Kafka or MQTT and emit tuples into the topology.
g.text: • Entry point of data in topology

g.data-narration: Bolts process input streams and produce new streams - they contain the core processing logic like filtering and aggregation.
Tuples are the basic data unit in Storm - named lists of values with optional anchor to input tuples for tracking.
g.text: • Basic unit of data in Storm

g.data-narration: Storm guarantees at-least-once processing through tuple tree tracking and ack/fail mechanisms with configurable timeouts.
Stream groupings control tuple distribution - shuffle for load balancing, fields for aggregations, all for broadcasting.
Storm provides true streaming with sub-second latency while Spark Streaming uses micro-batches with seconds of delay.
Key points: Nimbus masters task distribution, supervisors run workers, spouts emit tuples, bolts process them with various groupings.
Apache Storm enables real-time IoT analytics for equipment monitoring, anomaly detection, instant alerts, and live dashboards.
g.text: IoT Analytics • Equipment Failure Detection • Smart Grid Monitoring • Fraud Detection
